name: High-Frequency Offline RAG Expansion Pack
description: High-frequency symptom, medicine, hydration, respiratory, digestive, urinary, pain, and home-monitoring records tuned for stronger offline general and medicine retrieval.
reviewStatus: local pack requires clinician, pharmacy, and safety review before production medical use

records.title: Headache with Blood Pressure and Neurologic Warning Review
records.summary: Organizes headache using onset, location, blood pressure, hydration, sleep, medicine timing, vision change, nausea, and sudden neurologic symptoms before choosing the next safe step.
records.safetyNotes: Sudden severe headache, weakness, speech trouble, fainting, seizure, severe high blood pressure symptoms, or new confusion needs urgent real-world assessment.
whatToTrack: When the headache started, whether it was sudden or gradual, where it is located, and whether it is improving or worsening.
Repeat BP if available, recent sleep, hydration, stress, caffeine, food intake, and medicine timing.
Associated symptoms such as nausea, light sensitivity, vision change, numbness, weakness, speech change, or neck stiffness.
careQuestions: Which headache features suggest routine review versus same-day or urgent care?
How should blood pressure, dehydration, missed food, or medicine timing change the follow-up threshold?
precautions: Do not treat a sudden severe headache or new neurologic symptom as a routine stress headache.
Do not make blood-pressure dose changes from one headache episode without clinician direction.

records.title: Dizziness with Blood Pressure, Glucose, and Medicine Timing Review
records.summary: Builds a dizziness review around standing position, hydration, blood pressure, glucose, missed meals, heat, infection, recent medicines, and fall risk.
records.safetyNotes: Fainting, chest pain, severe breathing trouble, new weakness, black stool, repeated vomiting, or confusion needs urgent medical review.
whatToTrack: Whether dizziness happens at rest, after standing, after medicines, after missed food, or during illness.
Fluid intake, BP or glucose readings if available, recent fever, diarrhea, vomiting, and temperature exposure.
Any fall, near-fall, fainting, chest pressure, palpitations, or neurologic changes.
careQuestions: Could dehydration, low food intake, medicine timing, or BP change explain this pattern?
Which symptoms or readings mean this should move out of routine monitoring?
precautions: Avoid driving, ladders, and risky activity until the dizziness pattern is clearer and you feel steady.
Do not ignore dizziness when it comes with fainting, chest symptoms, or new neurologic change.

records.title: Cough, Fever, and Home Respiratory Review
records.summary: Structures cough and fever using breathing effort, sputum change, oxygen wording, hydration, fever trend, sick contacts, and home monitoring before follow-up planning.
records.safetyNotes: Breathing trouble, low oxygen, blue lips, chest pain, confusion, dehydration, or fast worsening illness needs prompt medical care.
whatToTrack: Temperature pattern, cough frequency, sputum color or amount, hydration, and sleep disruption.
Any oxygen reading if available, breathing effort, wheeze, chest discomfort, and ability to speak normally.
Duration, exposure history, home medicines taken, and whether symptoms are improving or worsening.
careQuestions: What warning signs separate routine viral review from same-day evaluation?
Which readings or symptoms matter most if cough and fever continue or worsen?
precautions: Do not rely on home monitoring alone when breathing is getting harder or oxygen is low.
Escalate sooner for chest pain, confusion, dehydration, or rapidly rising concern.

records.title: Sore Throat, Swallowing, and Hydration Review
records.summary: Organizes sore-throat questions using fever, cough, swallowing difficulty, voice change, neck swelling, hydration, and exposure history while keeping airway warning signs visible.
records.safetyNotes: Drooling, trouble breathing, inability to swallow fluids, severe neck swelling, dehydration, or rapidly worsening symptoms needs urgent assessment.
whatToTrack: Fever, cough, runny nose, swallowing difficulty, and whether fluids are going down normally.
Voice changes, drooling, neck swelling, bad breath, or one-sided pain.
Hydration, urine output, home pain relief used, and illness duration.
careQuestions: Which throat symptoms suggest routine follow-up versus a same-day airway check?
What should be tracked if the sore throat is not improving after hydration and rest?
precautions: Do not delay care when swallowing is failing or breathing feels affected.
Watch for dehydration if pain prevents normal fluid intake.

records.title: Urinary Burning, Frequency, and Flank-Pain Review
records.summary: Builds a urinary review around burning, urgency, frequency, fever, flank pain, blood in urine, hydration, pregnancy context, and prior UTI history.
records.safetyNotes: Fever with flank pain, vomiting, pregnancy concerns, confusion, dehydration, or inability to pass urine needs prompt medical review.
whatToTrack: When the urinary symptoms started, whether there is fever, flank pain, or visible blood.
Fluid intake, urine amount, color, smell, and whether symptoms are getting worse.
Pregnancy context, prior stones or UTIs, vomiting, and whether pain is one-sided.
careQuestions: Which urinary symptoms suggest simple follow-up versus same-day review for kidney involvement?
What details about fever, flank pain, pregnancy, or dehydration matter most for the next safe step?
precautions: Do not minimize urinary symptoms when fever, flank pain, or vomiting is also present.
Use urgent care sooner if urine flow drops sharply or confusion appears.

records.title: Acid Reflux, Meal Timing, and Chest-Burning Review
records.summary: Explains reflux-style symptoms using meal timing, trigger foods, lying down, cough, burping, chest-burning pattern, and medicine timing while separating emergency chest symptoms.
records.safetyNotes: Crushing chest pain, breathing trouble, black stool, vomiting blood, trouble swallowing, or unplanned weight loss needs real-world medical review.
whatToTrack: Which foods, meal size, position, or late eating pattern trigger or worsen symptoms.
Whether symptoms are mainly throat, chest, upper stomach, or nighttime cough related.
Any black stool, vomiting, swallowing difficulty, weight loss, or chest pressure pattern.
careQuestions: Which features fit reflux-style irritation and which need broader evaluation?
How should meal timing, weight, medicines, or nighttime symptoms affect follow-up?
precautions: Do not assume all chest burning is reflux if pressure, sweating, faintness, or breathlessness is present.
Escalate sooner for bleeding, swallowing trouble, or persistent worsening.

records.title: Vomiting, Diarrhea, and Dehydration Review
records.summary: Organizes stomach illness using fluid loss, vomiting frequency, diarrhea pattern, urine output, fever, blood, medicines, and glucose or blood-pressure context.
records.safetyNotes: Blood in stool or vomit, severe weakness, confusion, fainting, no urine, major abdominal pain, or inability to keep fluids down needs urgent evaluation.
whatToTrack: Number of vomiting or diarrhea episodes, whether fluids stay down, and when urine was last passed normally.
Fever, severe pain, blood, sick contacts, food exposure, and whether symptoms are improving.
Glucose, blood pressure, dizziness, and current medicines when chronic conditions are present.
careQuestions: What dehydration signals mean this should move beyond home monitoring?
Which chronic-condition or medicine issues should be raised early if vomiting or diarrhea continues?
precautions: Do not wait on routine advice if fluids are not staying down or urine output falls sharply.
Escalate for blood, confusion, fainting, or severe abdominal pain.

records.title: Sinus Pressure, Congestion, and Allergy Review
records.summary: Structures sinus and allergy symptoms using congestion, facial pressure, fever, discharge, ear pressure, cough, trigger exposure, and home symptom pattern.
records.safetyNotes: Eye swelling, severe headache, confusion, breathing trouble, facial swelling, or rapidly worsening symptoms needs urgent medical review.
whatToTrack: Whether symptoms follow allergy exposure, weather change, illness, or a longer congestion pattern.
Fever, facial pain, colored drainage, sleep disruption, ear pressure, and hydration.
Any vision issue, severe headache, swelling, or breathing change.
careQuestions: Which details help separate likely irritation or allergy from a higher-risk infection review?
What warning signs matter most if face pressure or congestion is getting worse?
precautions: Do not dismiss severe headache, swelling, or eye symptoms as simple congestion.
Use a more urgent path if breathing trouble or confusion is present.

records.title: Back Strain, Sciatica, and Neurologic Warning Review
records.summary: Organizes back pain using injury timing, lifting, leg symptoms, numbness, weakness, fever, bladder or bowel change, and daily function impact.
records.safetyNotes: Major injury, new weakness, numbness around the groin, bladder or bowel change, fever with severe pain, or inability to walk needs urgent assessment.
whatToTrack: Where the pain is located, what movement worsens it, and whether it spreads to the leg or foot.
Any numbness, weakness, fall, fever, bladder change, bowel change, or walking difficulty.
Home measures used, sleep disruption, and whether pain is stable, improving, or worsening.
careQuestions: Which symptoms fit a routine strain review and which require faster neurologic evaluation?
What function changes should be shared early if pain is not improving?
precautions: Do not ignore back pain when weakness, numbness, or bladder symptoms are present.
Use urgent care for severe injury, fever with pain, or inability to walk.

records.title: Fatigue with Anemia, Thyroid, Sleep, and Illness Review
records.summary: Builds a fatigue review around sleep, stress, mood, fever, anemia language, thyroid language, weight change, medicines, and activity tolerance.
records.safetyNotes: Chest pain, fainting, black stool, severe breathlessness, confusion, or rapidly worsening weakness needs real-world medical review.
whatToTrack: Sleep hours, snoring, stress, appetite, fever, and whether the fatigue is new or long standing.
Any palpitations, breathlessness, dizziness, bleeding history, weight change, or recent lab results.
Medicine changes, daily function impact, and whether rest improves symptoms.
careQuestions: Which combinations of fatigue plus other symptoms suggest lab review, sleep review, or more urgent assessment?
What home readings, report values, or history details would sharpen the next safe step?
precautions: Do not treat severe weakness, bleeding signals, or breathlessness as routine tiredness.
Escalate sooner if the fatigue is rapidly worsening or paired with fainting or chest symptoms.

records.title: Diabetes Foot Wound, Redness, and Sensation Review
records.summary: Organizes diabetes foot concerns using wound timing, redness, warmth, drainage, glucose pattern, sensation loss, shoe pressure, and infection warning signs.
records.safetyNotes: Rapidly spreading redness, foul drainage, fever, black tissue, severe swelling, or a new foot wound with diabetes needs prompt real-world evaluation.
whatToTrack: When the wound or redness was first seen, whether it is enlarging, and whether drainage or bad smell is present.
Glucose control pattern, walking pain, numbness, swelling, and shoe or pressure exposure.
Any fever, streaking redness, black tissue, or reduced ability to bear weight.
careQuestions: Which wound features need same-day review rather than watchful home care?
What details about glucose, drainage, numbness, and pressure points matter for follow-up?
precautions: Do not keep a diabetes foot wound on a routine path if redness is spreading or drainage appears.
Use prompt in-person review for black tissue, fever, or fast worsening.

records.title: Metformin Stomach Upset and Sick-Day Precaution Review
records.summary: Structures metformin questions around stomach upset, food timing, dehydration, vomiting, diarrhea, kidney context, and when pharmacist or clinician review is needed.
records.safetyNotes: Severe vomiting, dehydration, confusion, fainting, or worsening illness should move beyond routine medicine review.
whatToTrack: Whether the stomach symptoms happen after each dose, with specific meals, or mainly during illness.
Fluid intake, vomiting or diarrhea episodes, glucose readings, and kidney-related history.
Any inability to keep fluids down, worsening weakness, or major appetite drop.
careQuestions: What label, food-timing, or pharmacist questions matter most for metformin stomach symptoms?
Which illness or dehydration signals mean the medicine question should be escalated sooner?
precautions: Do not make personal dose changes from app advice alone.
Escalate beyond routine review when vomiting, dehydration, confusion, or severe weakness is present.

records.title: Amlodipine Swelling and Dizziness Review
records.summary: Organizes amlodipine questions using ankle swelling, dizziness on standing, BP pattern, heat, hydration, salt intake, and fall-risk context.
records.safetyNotes: Fainting, chest pain, severe breathing trouble, rapidly worsening swelling, or one-sided leg symptoms needs prompt medical review.
whatToTrack: When swelling or dizziness happens in relation to the dose, heat, standing, travel, or salt-heavy meals.
BP readings, weight change, breathlessness, and whether swelling is both sides or one side.
Any fall, fainting, chest symptoms, or severe weakness.
careQuestions: Which swelling or dizziness features are common discussion points and which need faster review?
What BP, weight, or symptom pattern should be shared with the next clinician or pharmacist discussion?
precautions: Do not stop the medicine on your own because of mild symptoms without pharmacist or clinician review.
Use urgent assessment for fainting, severe breathlessness, or rapidly worsening swelling.

records.title: Losartan Missed Dose and NSAID Kidney-Safety Review
records.summary: Builds losartan safety questions around missed-dose wording, ibuprofen or NSAID use, kidney context, BP follow-up, dehydration, and urine change.
records.safetyNotes: Severe dizziness, fainting, very low urine, major swelling, or fast worsening illness needs real-world medical review.
whatToTrack: When the dose was missed, whether the next scheduled dose is close, and what the label says.
Any ibuprofen or NSAID use, kidney history, urine amount, dizziness, and BP pattern.
Fluid intake, vomiting or diarrhea, and whether symptoms are new since the medicine timing issue.
careQuestions: What label or pharmacist questions matter when a missed dose and NSAID use happen together?
Which urine, dizziness, or BP changes should raise the urgency of follow-up?
precautions: Do not double or catch up a dose from app guidance.
Escalate sooner if urine output falls, fainting occurs, or illness is causing dehydration.

records.title: Ibuprofen, Kidney, and Stomach-Protection Review
records.summary: Organizes ibuprofen-style medicine questions using stomach irritation, kidney history, dehydration, bleeding signals, blood-pressure context, and safer follow-up questions.
records.safetyNotes: Black stool, vomiting blood, severe abdominal pain, allergic swelling, very low urine, or confusion needs urgent medical review.
whatToTrack: How often the medicine was used, whether it was taken with illness or poor fluid intake, and any stomach pain or reflux change.
Urine output, swelling, BP pattern, and any history of kidney disease or ulcer problems.
Any black stool, vomiting blood, rash, face swelling, or severe weakness.
careQuestions: Which stomach, kidney, or bleeding questions should be raised with a pharmacist or clinician?
What other patient-specific context makes NSAID use more concerning?
precautions: Do not keep using this pattern without review if bleeding or kidney-warning signals appear.
Escalate urgently for black stool, blood, very low urine, or confusion.

records.title: Constipation, Fiber, Fluid, and Red-Flag Review
records.summary: Structures constipation using bowel pattern, fiber and fluid intake, new medicines, bloating, abdominal pain, vomiting, and bleeding signals.
records.safetyNotes: Severe abdominal pain, vomiting, black stool, blood, inability to pass stool or gas, or rapid worsening needs medical review.
whatToTrack: When the bowel pattern changed, how long it has been, and whether gas is also reduced.
Fiber, fluid intake, travel, activity, and any new medicines or iron use.
Any severe pain, vomiting, blood, black stool, or abdominal swelling.
careQuestions: Which constipation features stay routine and which need a faster review?
What diet, fluid, medicine, and timing details would clarify the next safe step?
precautions: Do not minimize constipation when vomiting, severe pain, or bleeding is present.
Escalate sooner if stool and gas both stop or the abdomen becomes more distended.
